data.title: Jeff Schmitt Reiterates Buy as Strong Q1, Commonwealth Synergies and Recruiting Momentum Drive Compelling EPS Growth Outlook Through 2027
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jeff Schmitt has maintained their bullish stance on LPLA stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jeff Schmitt has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including solid first-quarter performance and improving growth visibility. Earnings modestly topped market expectations, with revenue supported by higher client assets from the Commonwealth acquisition and better spread income as client cash balances recover, while expense control and resumed share repurchases enhance profitability.

He also points to a strengthening advisor recruiting pipeline, which should translate into better organic growth in the second half of the year and beyond. Schmitt forecasts roughly 20% average EPS growth in 2026 and 2027, driven by the Commonwealth transaction, accelerating organic growth, higher spread income, ongoing cost discipline, and buybacks, and he notes that shares trade at a low multiple of his 2027 estimate, making the risk-reward compelling.

Schmitt covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Tradeweb Markets, Marketaxess Holdings, and Nasdaq. According to TipRanks, Schmitt has an average return of 8.6% and a 54.13% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: FLSmidth & Co. A/S: Buy Rating and $610 Price Target Reaffirmed Despite Russia-Related Sanctions Overhang
data.description:
Analyst David Farrell of Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on FLSmidth & Co. A/S, retaining the price target of DKK610.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Farrell has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, despite the announcement of a potential breach of Russia-related sanctions tied to tender activity in Kazakhstan. He notes that Kazakhstan represents only a minor contribution to FLSmidth & Co. A/S’s overall business, and the company’s latest large original equipment order from that market has already been delivered, limiting direct financial impact.

Farrell also emphasizes that there is no change to the firm’s 2026 financial outlook or to its intention to start a share buyback in the second quarter, which signals management confidence in the underlying business. While he acknowledges that the issue may weigh on investor sentiment and slightly dampen order prospects, he views it mainly as a reputational overhang rather than a fundamental threat, and therefore keeps his price target unchanged at $610, supporting his Buy recommendation.

In another report released on April 20, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a DKK610.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Zeta Maintained at Buy as Strong Q1, Marigold Outperformance and Athena AI Momentum Support Unchanged $25 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Scott Berg of Needham maintained a Buy rating on Zeta Global Holdings Corp, retaining the price target of $25.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Scott Berg has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Zeta’s strong first-quarter performance and momentum in its core marketing platform, which delivered an acceleration in organic revenue growth from the prior quarter as the offering gains broader customer adoption. He also highlights that the recently acquired Marigold business exceeded expectations by a meaningful margin, contributing to Zeta’s best revenue beat to date and reinforcing confidence in the company’s ability to execute on acquisitions.

In addition, Berg points to early but compelling signs of success from the Athena AI solution, which helped secure Zeta’s largest contract so far and indicates that AI is becoming a key growth driver for the platform. He further notes that average revenue per super-scaled customer rose at its fastest pace in more than a year, reflecting healthy expansion within the existing base, while full-year 2026 revenue guidance appears conservative relative to current growth trends, supporting his unchanged $25 price target and Buy recommendation.

In another report released on April 28, KeyBanc also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a $22.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Exponent Maintained at Buy as Strong Q1 Beat and Durable Growth Drivers Support Unchanged $66.89 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Andrew Nicholas of William Blair reiterated a Buy rating on Exponent, retaining the price target of $66.89.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Andrew Nicholas’s rating is based on Exponent’s strong first-quarter outperformance and the durability of its growth drivers. The company exceeded his expectations on net revenue, profitability, and earnings per share, while management maintained full-year guidance, reinforcing confidence in the outlook and justifying his unchanged $66.89 price target.

Andrew Nicholas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of robust demand across both reactive and proactive services and clear benefits from AI-related work. He highlights especially strong momentum in consumer electronics, diversified across health-related devices, AI-enabled products, and hardware, which, alongside secular tailwinds and a reduced revenue concentration, should help smooth out cyclical swings and support continued double-digit top-line growth.
